Mechanical Definition / Rule Parameter: Applies strictly to the current target mechanic execution or roll resolution, leaving no lingering state or tracking requirements behind[cite: 1].
Persists until a specific structural phase checkpoint inside the current round loop is completed, such as End of Active Turn[cite: 1], Start of Round[cite: 1], or Round Closure[cite: 1].
Establishes a persistent state that remains active continuously until an explicit Standard Action (like a Manipulate action) or state-removal test clears it[cite: 1].
Persists for a fixed integer value of rounds (X Rounds), decrementing during the round closure phase until reaching 0.
Alters the rule state continuously from the exact moment of activation until the formal Combat End transition phase resolves[cite: 1].
Legally limits the activation of a block to a set maximum number of iterations per combat round, resetting at Round Start[cite: 1].
Legally limits the activation of a block to a set maximum number of iterations within a single tactical battle encounter.
Grants a finite pool of uses (X charges) across an entire exploration map, resetting only when returning to the Lair Phase[cite: 1].
The block has a fixed, absolute total number of uses across its lifetime[cite: 1]. Once the final charge hits 0, the block is void or the chassis breaks permanently[cite: 1].
Active exclusively during abstract wilderness map travel, clearing or resetting automatically upon arrival at a node stage[cite: 1].
Equipment tags or modifications persist through all nodes and skirmishes for the entire deployment, falling apart during Homecoming[cite: 1].
Remains active strictly during the 1-week downtime phase back at base, requiring reallocation or resetting when the next raid launches[cite: 1].
Stays active continuously across all game sessions, hard-coded to the current PC until they die or transition into active retirement as an Elder[cite: 1].
Irreversibly alters global baseline game rules, tracking across generations of characters directly on the global Lair or Gang sheets[cite: 1].
